item.title: Russia’s Flagship Gas Pipeline to China Remains in Xi’s Hands
item.description: A pipeline deal will be on the agenda when Russia’s president visits Beijing this week.

item.title: Markets Are Undervaluing Video Game Talent and IP, Investor Says
item.description: Kensei Capital founder wants to help game makers scale up

item.title: Bond Jitters Test G-7 Finance Chiefs’ Nerves in Paris
item.description: An alarming debt-market selloff is intruding on a meeting of finance ministers.

item.title: Trump-Xi ‘Placeholder’ Summit Leaves Room for Tensions to Reignite
item.description: The meeting was long on good vibes. The question now is, how long will they last?

item.title: SpaceX’s Mega-IPO Puts a Price Tag on the Fear of Missing Out
item.description: Elon Musk has turned science fiction into reality for the better part of two decades, making believers rich and earning the benefit of the doubt from many on Wall Street. Away from rockets and electric cars, his companies are bending reality on another plane – convincing analysts to stretch their traditional valuation tools, and assign multiples based on what one academic calls “optionalities.”

item.title: IMF Tells UK to Stick to Fiscal Plan as Starmer Rivals Circle
item.description: The International Monetary Fund urged the UK to stick to plans to reduce borrowing and warned of limited space for tax increases as investors brace for a challenge to Keir Starmer’s premiership that could signal changes in economic policy.

item.title: Reasons Why Investors Are Selling Government Bonds From UK to US
item.description: Bonds are buckling around the world, propelling borrowing costs to multi-year highs as concerns over war-driven inflation and government spending mount.

item.title: Yield Surge Puts Logic Behind Stocks Rally to the Test
item.description: Equities are showing signs of resilience even as yields jump.

item.title: India Weighing Edible Oil Import Duty Hike to Support Farmers
item.description: India is considering a request by the domestic vegetable oil industry to raise import duties, potentially increasing taxes on another major commodity just after a hike in gold tariffs.

item.title: European LNG Imports Extend April’s Drop as Flows to Asia Climb
item.description: Europe’s imports of liquefied natural gas are heading for a second month of declines, as the Iran war disrupts flows.

item.title: Burnham Gives Investors Mixed Message Ahead of UK Leadership Race
item.description: One of Andy Burnham’s biggest tests in his bid to become UK prime minister is whether he can calm investors who are concerned he would increase public borrowing.

item.title: AI Is Shifting the Corporate Pyramid in Favor of Older Workers
item.description: The shift is poised to be strongest in Europe and Asia.

item.title: Can AI Drug Development Live Up to the Hype?
item.description: Big Pharma is pouring money into tech-devised treatments, but the verdict is out on whether they’ll actually work.

item.title: Lufthansa Speaks to Investors for Euro Bond as Fuel Stress Grows
item.description: Deutsche Lufthansa AG is speaking with investors ahead of a potential euro-denominated bond sale, as the airline industry grapples with rising jet fuel costs.

item.title: IEA Chief Warns Commercial Oil Inventories Are Falling Very Fast
item.description: International Energy Agency Executive Director Fatih Birol warned that the tally of commercial oil inventories is shrinking at an accelerated pace.

item.title: DraftKings, Meta, AI Firms Have a New Election Playbook: Flood State-Level Races With Cash
item.description: With Congress largely stalemated, companies turn to states to gain leverage over regulations.

item.title: Elon Musk Really Needs Starship to Work This Time
item.description: With the V3 launch, SpaceX hopes to prove that the massive vehicle it’s betting everything on will perform as advertised.

item.title: US, Philippines Moving ‘Very Quickly’ on 4,000-Acre AI Hub Plan
item.description: The US and the Philippines are moving “very, very quickly” on a planned artificial intelligence and supply chain hub in the Southeast Asian nation, a top US official said, as Washington seeks long-term protection for American investors.

item.title: Tanker Tally at Iran’s Kharg Island Hits Post-Blockade Peak
item.description: Some 23 tankers have been spotted around Iran’s principal oil-export hub, the largest cluster to have gathered at the island since the US Navy began a blockade on the country’s ports a month ago, interdicting and seizing ships.

item.title: Court Challenge Puts Thailand’s Crisis Loan Plan in Doubt
item.description: Thailand’s Constitutional Court will hear a challenge to the government’s 400 billion baht ($12 billion) emergency borrowing plan, casting uncertainty over its response to rising energy costs.

item.title: Ukraine Says It’s Unsure of US Stance on Russia Oil Sanctions
item.description: Ukraine remains uncertain about the US position on Russian oil, even after Washington let its waiver for purchases of Russian crude expire.

item.title: Baidu AI Sales Eclipse Waning Legacy Ads for the First Time
item.description: Baidu Inc. posted a better-than-feared 1% drop in revenue after growth in nascent AI businesses offset a steady decline in traditional internet revenue, buying the search leader time to catch rivals such as Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. in the post-ChatGPT era.

item.title: Mineral Resources Restarts Australia Lithium Mine as Prices Rise
item.description: Mineral Resources Ltd. will restart its Bald Hill lithium mine in Western Australia after an 18-month hiatus, betting that a rebound in prices of the battery material will support renewed production.

item.title: StanChart to Absorb Zodia Subsidiary’s Crypto Custody Business
item.description: Standard Chartered Plc plans to acquire the crypto custody business of its majority-owned subsidiary Zodia Custody Ltd., expanding the reach of the bank’s own digital-asset operations.

item.title: Korea’s Homeplus, Meritz Tussle Over $67 Million Loan Terms
item.description: South Korean retailer Homeplus Co. is objecting to a condition tied to a roughly 100 billion won ($67 million) bridge loan from its largest creditor Meritz Financial Group, placing the emergency funding at risk.

item.title: Morgan Stanley’s Wilson Warns Stock Rally at Risk From Bond Rout
item.description: Equities are at risk of a significant pullback as the global bond selloff threatens to derail the artificial intelligence-driven rally, according to Morgan Stanley strategists.

item.title: UK Bonds Steady, With 10-Year Yield Holding Near Multi-Year High
item.description: UK bonds edged higher on Monday, though yields remained near multi-year highs as investors weighed the fiscal implications of Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham potentially challenging for UK Prime Minister.

item.title: Kalshi, Polymarket Defy India Ban on Online Betting Platforms
item.description: Polymarket and Kalshi Inc. have been allowing customers in India to sign up and trade on their prediction markets even after the country’s technology ministry warned that the platforms are illegal.

item.title: Kioxia’s Booming Shares Head for Liquidity Boost With US Float
item.description: Kioxia Holdings Corp.’s plan to float American Depositary Shares is setting one of the world’s hottest stocks up for a liquidity boost, tapping into the global fervor over memory chips to expand its investor base.

item.title: Elon Musk Says He Wants to Get SpaceX IPO Underway ‘Pretty Soon’
item.description: Elon Musk said he’s back in Texas working on plans for an initial public offering of SpaceX as the rocket and artificial intelligence company gears up for the largest listing of all time.
